forecast-period.text: A cold front will move across South Australia today. A weak high pressure system will immediately follow the cold front and drift eastwards to be over Victoria on Sunday. The high will move over the Tasman Sea on Monday with a northerly flow increasing over SA ahead of a low pressure system that is expected south of the SA coast next week.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

A band of showers or patchy rain moving across the state, gradually clearing from the south during the afternoon and contracting to the Pastoral and Flinders districts in the evening. Remaining dry in the far northeast. Maximum temperatures near average grading to well above average in the northeast and below average about the far west. North to northwesterly winds ahead of a south to southwesterly change extending into the North East Pastoral district during the afternoon.

Isolated showers across the pastoral districts, becoming more frequent about the North West Pastoral during the day and extending into the West Coast during the evening. Morning fog patches on and east of the ranges. Possible thunderstorms about the far west later. Maximum temperatures near average grading to above average in the north and below average about the far west. Light winds tending southeast to northeasterly.

Showers about the south of the North West Pastoral district and the West Coast, tending to patchy rain and extending into central and southeastern districts during the day. Possible thunderstorms about the west. Maximum temperatures near average grading to above average in the north. North to northeasterly winds ahead of a west to southwesterly change moving across the far west late in the evening.

text.p: Areas of rain and isolated thunderstorms over western parts and the southern agricultural area, tending to isolated showers reaching the northern agricultural area and the North East Pastoral district. Maximum temperatures near to above average tending slightly below average in the west. Freshening northerly winds ahead of a westerly change moving over the far west later.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Tuesday: Generally 10 to 30 mm grading to 2 to 10 mm northeast of about Pukatja/Ernabella to Yunta, and dry about the far northeast. Areas of 30 to 60 mm are likely in a band from the far west of the North West Pastoral district stretching over western and central agricultural areas including the Adelaide Hills.
